tasks.description: Phase B Step 1: Add force_reconcile_payment_to_confirmed and force_reconcile_payment_to_failed to src/common/db_handler.py. Both accept (payment_id, *, tx_signature, reason, guild_id) with allowed_statuses=['submitted','processing','failed','manual_hold'] — explicitly broader than mark_payment_confirmed/mark_payment_failed's strict guards because reconciliation corrects history from authoritative on-chain truth. Append to tx_signature_history with reason='reconcile_confirmed' or 'reconcile_failed'. Reject pending_confirmation/queued/cancelled starting states (never reconcile-forward a row that never reached the chain). Log at WARNING with before/after status. Do NOT modify existing mark_payment_confirmed/mark_payment_failed — their strict guards remain the normal state-machine contract. Add unit tests in tests/test_admin_payments.py (or new tests/test_payment_reconcile.py) covering each allowed starting state, each rejected starting state, history append, and WARNING log.

Phase B Step 2: Add PaymentService.reconcile_with_chain(payment_id, *, guild_id) -> ReconcileDecision in src/features/payments/payment_service.py. Define frozen dataclass ReconcileDecision{decision: Literal['reconciled_confirmed','reconciled_failed','allow_requeue','keep_in_hold','not_applicable'], reason: str, tx_signature: Optional[str]}. Logic: fetch row; no tx_signature → allow_requeue('no prior signature'); missing provider → keep_in_hold('provider unavailable'); provider.check_status returns 'confirmed' → call force_reconcile_payment_to_confirmed → reconciled_confirmed; 'failed' → force_reconcile_payment_to_failed → reconciled_failed; 'not_found' AND now-submitted_at > 150s → allow_requeue('beyond 150s blockhash safety window'); 'not_found' within 150s → keep_in_hold('too recent'); provider exception OR 'rpc_unreachable' sentinel → keep_in_hold('RPC unreachable during reconcile'). Do NOT mutate state on allow_requeue/keep_in_hold. Unit-test all six branches with fake provider and fake DB.

Phase B Step 3: Thread payment_service through admin_chat retry/release tools in src/features/admin_chat/tools.py. Change execute_retry_payment and execute_release_payment to accept bot; resolve payment_service = getattr(bot, 'payment_service', None); fail closed with {'success': False, 'error': 'payment_service unavailable'} if missing. Update dispatcher at tools.py:3134-3139 to pass bot for both (mirror the existing initiate_payment pattern at tools.py:3142-3143). Gate execute_retry_payment on await payment_service.reconcile_with_chain(payment_id, guild_id=guild_id): reconciled_confirmed/reconciled_failed → return reconciled row, skip requeue; allow_requeue → proceed to db_handler.requeue_payment; keep_in_hold → call db_handler.mark_payment_manual_hold with decision.reason and return non-success. Gate execute_release_payment the same way — reconciled outcomes refuse the requested release_payment_hold and return reconciled truth; allow_requeue proceeds to release_payment_hold; keep_in_hold calls mark_payment_manual_hold. Add tests covering five branches per tool with fake payment_service.

Phase B Step 4: Add /payment-resolve slash command in src/features/payments/payment_cog.py (or post-cleanup PaymentUICog). Use discord.app_commands.Command with payment_id:str parameter. Gate on ADMIN_USER_ID check INSIDE the callback (match repo's admin gating pattern, not a permission decorator). On non-admin caller: reply ephemeral 'admin-only' and return. Body: decision = await self.payment_service.reconcile_with_chain(payment_id, guild_id=interaction.guild_id); reply ephemeral with structured embed/code block showing decision.decision, decision.reason, new status, redacted signature. CRITICAL: do NOT expose this as an admin_chat LLM tool — reconciliation surface must stay off the LLM path to preserve the hardening-pass prompt-injection closure. No execute_payment_resolve function anywhere in src/features/admin_chat/tools.py. Step 1's force_reconcile_* already writes tx_signature_history, so no extra audit append here. Unit-test the callback against fake payment_service and fake interaction for each decision branch.

Phase B Step 5: Provider name migration path. Add db_handler.get_legacy_provider_payment_requests(guild_ids) that selects rows where provider='solana'. Add PaymentService.migrate_legacy_provider_rows(guild_ids) -> int: for each legacy row, producer='grants' → update provider='solana_grants'; producer='admin_chat' → 'solana_payouts'; any other producer → db_handler.mark_payment_manual_hold with reason='legacy provider could not be mapped: unknown producer={producer}'. Log one WARNING per row with payment_id, before, after. Call it once at worker startup from PaymentWorkerCog.cog_load (post-cleanup) or PaymentCog.cog_load if cleanup hasn't landed. Test with fake DB containing three legacy rows (grants, admin_chat, unknown) — assert two rewrites and one manual_hold.

Phase C Step 6: Dynamic priority fees in src/features/grants/solana_client.py (the shared SolanaClient used by both providers — do NOT create a new file). Add SolanaClient._get_dynamic_priority_fee(client) -> int: call client.get_recent_prioritization_fees() (verify exact SDK method at impl time, fall through to raw RPC via provider._provider_core.make_request if needed); compute 75th percentile of non-zero prioritization_fee values; clamp floor=self.priority_fee_micro_lamports, ceiling=int(os.getenv('SOLANA_PRIORITY_FEE_CEILING_MICRO_LAMPORTS', '1000000')). On exception or empty response: return self.priority_fee_micro_lamports and log WARNING with error string. Use the result at solana_client.py:94-98 inside send_sol, replacing static compute_unit_price = self.priority_fee_micro_lamports; log chosen value alongside existing priority-fee log. Test in tests/test_solana_client.py: mock SDK call; assert percentile math, floor clamp, ceiling clamp, fallback-on-error.

Phase C Step 7: Admin DM fallback channel in src/features/payments/payment_cog.py (or post-cleanup PaymentWorkerCog). Read ADMIN_FALLBACK_CHANNEL_ID env var in worker cog __init__. Extract helper _deliver_admin_alert(message: str) and route both _dm_admin_payment_success (payment_cog.py:288-344) and _dm_admin_payment_failure (payment_cog.py:346-406) through it: try DM via fetch_user(ADMIN_USER_ID).send(message); on discord.Forbidden or discord.HTTPException with rate-limit status → resolve ADMIN_FALLBACK_CHANNEL_ID and post via safe_send_message; if both fail → logger.error('[PaymentCog] admin alert undeliverable', extra={'message_preview': message[:120]}). Test: fake bot where DM raises Forbidden asserts fallback channel send called; fake bot where both fail asserts ERROR log.

Phase C Step 8: RPC-down vs timeout distinction. Add 'rpc_unreachable' sentinel to SolanaProvider.confirm_tx and check_status return values in src/features/payments/solana_provider.py. Emit it ONLY on connection-family exceptions: aiohttp.ClientConnectionError, aiohttp.ClientConnectorError, asyncio.TimeoutError during connect, solana.rpc.core.RPCException with transport cause (verify exact types at impl time). A legitimate confirmation timeout (wait-budget expired but RPC responsive) remains 'timeout'. Update PaymentService._confirm_submitted_payment (payment_service.py:548+) to branch on 'rpc_unreachable' → mark_payment_manual_hold with reason='rpc_unreachable: confirmation RPC offline' (distinct from 'Confirmation timed out after submission'). Update PaymentService.recover_inflight (payment_service.py:458+) same way for submitted branch. Bridge to T2: reconcile_with_chain already treats 'rpc_unreachable' as keep_in_hold per Step 2's spec. Test: fake provider returning 'rpc_unreachable' asserts distinct manual_hold reason in both call sites.

Phase A Step 9: Decompose PaymentService.request_payment (payment_service.py:69-316) into 5 named helpers with behavior and public signature IDENTICAL: _normalize_inputs(**kwargs) -> NormalizedRequest (absorbs lines 91-98 + provider key normalization at 139; returns None on validation failure); _detect_collision_and_idempotent_row(normalized, guild_id, is_test) -> tuple[Optional[row], Optional[row], Optional[row]] (absorbs lines 99-122); _derive_amounts(normalized, is_test, amount_usd, amount_token, provider) -> AmountsResult (absorbs lines 161-188); _enforce_caps(normalized, amount_token, amount_usd, provider) -> CapResult (absorbs lines 189-223); _persist_row(record, normalized, guild_id, is_test, collision_row, blocking_prior_row) -> Optional[dict] (absorbs lines 286-316 including duplicate re-read fallback). Rewrite request_payment as ~40-line thin orchestrator. Public signature and all log messages stay IDENTICAL — this is the behavior-preservation gate. Run existing test suite unchanged; it must pass with zero modifications.

Phase A Step 10: Move rent-exempt guard into PaymentService.__init__. Add module-level constants in src/features/payments/payment_service.py: RENT_EXEMPT_LAMPORTS = 890_880 and MIN_TEST_LAMPORTS = 2_000_000. In PaymentService.__init__, compute int(self.test_payment_amount * 1_000_000_000) and raise ValueError with the existing message from main.py:178-184 if below MIN_TEST_LAMPORTS. Remove the duplicate guard from main.py:173-184 keeping only the env var read. Unit test: PaymentService(test_payment_amount=0.0001, ...) raises ValueError.

Phase A Step 11: Consolidate _redact_wallet into NEW src/common/redaction.py (chain-agnostic, dedicated module per orchestrator decision #2 — NOT discord_utils.py). Create redact_wallet(wallet: Optional[str]) -> str with the same implementation currently duplicated in payment_service.py:33, payment_cog.py:18, and db_handler.py:13. Replace all three local definitions with `from src.common.redaction import redact_wallet as _redact_wallet` to minimize call-site churn. Do NOT touch admin_chat/tools.py's _redact_wallet_address, _redact_wallet_row, or _redact_payment_row — different signatures/behaviors, separate follow-up per orchestrator decision #3. Add trivial unit test on redact_wallet(None), redact_wallet('short'), redact_wallet('AbCdEfGhIjKlMnOpQrStUvWxYz1234567890').

Phase D Step 12: recover_inflight vs worker-loop race — formal argument + sanity check. Create tests/test_payment_race.py with verdict comment at the top citing Postgres semantics (NOT the test) as the proof: claim_due_payment_requests at sql/payments.sql:166-198 uses `where status='queued'` inside `FOR UPDATE SKIP LOCKED` CTE and updates claimed row to 'processing' atomically; under read-committed isolation, that transition is visible only after commit; recover_inflight only touches 'processing'/'submitted' rows. The verdict MUST label the test as 'logical-correctness sanity check, not a proof of DB-level concurrency safety' and MUST NOT say 'proven safe by this test'. Write test_recover_inflight_and_execute_payment_logical_ordering using fake DB with ordered-log recording on mark_payment_*; simulate (a) recover_inflight inspects processing then execute_payment transitions to submitted; (b) execute_payment mid-flight then recover_inflight runs; assert consistent terminal state across both orderings and no double confirmed marks. ONLY IF the sanity check surfaces a real gremlin: add conditional update to recover_inflight restricting reclamation to rows with updated_at older than 150 seconds (5× the 30s worker tick, per decision #5); update verdict to 'gremlin found; fixed in Step 12.3'.

Phase D Step 13: Concurrent admin-payment collision investigation. Target the DOWNSTREAM call sites, not execute_initiate_payment which dedupes per (guild_id, source_channel_id, recipient_user_id) at the admin_payment_intent table before producer_ref is set at tools.py:2486. Real surface is the downstream call to PaymentService.request_payment inside admin_chat_cog.py's _start_admin_payment_flow (~:246) and handle_payment_result (~:364) which use the intent's stored producer_ref. Write tests/test_admin_payments.py::test_concurrent_admin_payment_producer_ref_collision: patch time.time() to a fixed value; call execute_initiate_payment twice with DISTINCT source_channel_id (bypasses intent dedupe) and same (guild_id, recipient_user_id); drive both intents through _start_admin_payment_flow → PaymentService.request_payment at distinct amounts. Assert whether the second row collapses onto the first (gremlin) or collision logic at payment_service.py:99-122 rejects it. If confirmed (per decision #6): change producer_ref format at tools.py:2486 from f'{guild_id}_{recipient_user_id}_{int(time.time())}' to f'{guild_id}_{recipient_user_id}_{int(time.time() * 1000)}'. DO NOT use UUID suffix — rejected because it would break idempotency on legitimate replay paths. Commit test as regression guard with verdict comment: 'VERDICT 2026-04-11: collision reproduced; fixed by millisecond precision in producer_ref' OR 'VERDICT: collision refuted; this test is a regression guard.'

Phase D Step 14: Property-based state machine tests. Add `hypothesis` to requirements.txt. Create tests/test_payment_state_machine.py using hypothesis.stateful.RuleBasedStateMachine against a fake in-memory DatabaseHandler store. Rules: create_payment, confirm_payment, execute_payment, recover_inflight, release_payment_hold, requeue_payment, reconcile_with_chain (to cover T2's new method). Invariants checked after every rule: (1) every status='confirmed' row has non-null tx_signature AND fake provider check_status returns 'confirmed'; (2) no row transitions from terminal (confirmed, cancelled) back to queued/processing/submitted — reconciliation from failed/manual_hold via force_reconcile_* is ALLOWED and NOT treated as backward; (3) sum(amount_usd) where status='confirmed' and provider in capped_providers and completed_at within 24h <= daily_usd_cap + per_payment_usd_cap (slack allows one in-flight breach); (4) is_test rows have amount_usd is None and token_price_usd is None; (5) fail-closed: after execute_payment raises, row is never left in processing/submitted without subsequent manual_hold or failed reason. Run: `pytest tests/test_payment_state_machine.py --hypothesis-show-statistics`. Green suite OR gremlin caught with xfail-to-fix-to-pass cycle.

Phase E Step 15: docs/payments.md — new architecture doc. Include: (1) State machine ASCII diagram: pending_confirmation → queued → processing → submitted → {confirmed, failed, manual_hold, cancelled}; also failed→queued (requeue), manual_hold→failed (release), reconciliation edges from failed/manual_hold to confirmed/failed via force_reconcile_*. (2) Authorization model table of PaymentActorKind × producer, read from producer_flows.py:16-30; note AUTO is test-payment confirmer, RECIPIENT_CLICK/RECIPIENT_MESSAGE/ADMIN_DM are real-payment confirmers gated by producer. (3) Cap enforcement: per_payment_usd_cap + daily_usd_cap + capped_providers with file:line to payment_service.py:192-223 (post-refactor: to _enforce_caps). (4) Fail-closed contract: enumerated invariants with file:line pointers. (5) Reconciliation contract: force_reconcile_* bypasses normal transition guards on purpose because it corrects history from authoritative on-chain truth; only reachable via /payment-resolve slash command (never via LLM tool surface). (6) Non-obvious constraints: rent-exempt floor 0.002 SOL, static+dynamic priority fees, two-wallet split, idempotency index excludes failed/cancelled, millisecond-precision producer_ref for admin-initiated payments. Length target ≤400 lines.

Phase E Step 16: docs/runbook-payments.md — new operator runbook. Per-scenario playbooks: manual_hold → use /payment-resolve with decision tree per last_error prefix; failed → when to requeue via admin_chat retry_payment (now reconcile-gated), when to write off via release_payment_hold; wallet ghost-verified → SQL to clear verified_at and re-verify via new test payment; admin DM not received → check ADMIN_FALLBACK_CHANNEL_ID, escalation; RPC down → degraded-mode expectations (new 'rpc_unreachable' manual_hold reason), when to drain; budget cap hit → how to raise, how to audit recent spend. Include SQL audit queries from scripts/audit_ghost_confirmed_payments.py as copy-paste appendix. CRITICAL: include scheduler-wire-up section per orchestrator decision #4 documenting operator's choice between Railway cron, GitHub Actions scheduled workflow, or external scheduler for running scripts/check_payment_invariants.py daily. Explicitly flag as OPERATOR DECISION — this plan does NOT ship a CI workflow file or Railway config change.

Phase F Step 17: scripts/check_payment_invariants.py — new. Port scripts/audit_ghost_confirmed_payments.py as a superset. Implement all five checks: (a) every status='confirmed' solana row has tx_signature AND on-chain getSignatureStatuses returns err=null; (b) no row has been in pending_confirmation or processing for more than 24 hours (updated_at < now() - interval '24 hours'); (c) every wallet_registry row with verified_at has a corresponding confirmed is_test=true payment with amount_token >= 0.001 SOL; (d) no two wallet_registry rows share same wallet_address across different discord_user_id; (e) 24h rolling sum(amount_usd) where status='confirmed' and provider='solana_payouts' vs ADMIN_PAYOUT_DAILY_USD_CAP — warn at ≥90%, error at ≥100%. Exit nonzero on any violation; print structured report to stdout. Do NOT add a CI workflow file — scheduler wire-up lives in docs/runbook-payments.md only per decision #4. Test with synthetic-violation fixture seeded with one row violating each invariant; assert each violation is reported.

Phase F Step 18: Structured tx_confirm_decision log in src/features/grants/solana_client.py. Add structured logging at all three decision branches in SolanaClient.confirm_tx (solana_client.py:139-175): (1) Success branch after status inspection at line 169: logger.info('tx_confirm_decision', extra={'event':'tx_confirm_decision','signature':signature,'err':None,'slot':getattr(status,'slot',None),'confirmation_status':getattr(status,'confirmation_status',None),'decision':'confirmed'}); (2) Not-found branch around line 163: same schema with decision='not_found', err=None; (3) Errored branch around 169-172: same schema with decision='errored', err=repr(status.err). Test: patch logger, run confirm_tx under each branch, assert extra dict contains 'event':'tx_confirm_decision' and expected decision.

Final verification: run the full test suite and validate end-to-end. Commands: (1) `pytest tests/test_admin_payments.py tests/test_payment_reconcile.py -k 'reconcile or retry or release or resolve or migration'` to validate Phase B; (2) `pytest tests/test_solana_client.py tests/test_admin_payments.py -k 'priority or fallback or rpc_unreachable'` to validate Phase C; (3) `pytest` (full suite) to validate Phase A behavior preservation and overall regression; (4) `pytest tests/test_payment_race.py tests/test_payment_state_machine.py --hypothesis-show-statistics` to validate Phase D; inspect verdict comments in both files to confirm they cite Postgres semantics (not the test) for T12, and report T13 outcome clearly. (5) Run `python scripts/check_payment_invariants.py` against local test DB with seeded synthetic violations; confirm nonzero exit. If any test fails: read error, fix the code, re-run until green. Do NOT create new test files beyond the ones already specified in T1-T18 — run the project's existing suite plus the new suites. Additionally, write a short throwaway script that reproduces a specific reconcile-with-chain scenario end-to-end (e.g., failed row with prior signature → reconcile_with_chain → verify row now shows reconciled_confirmed with tx_signature_history entry), run it to confirm the fix works, then delete the script.

watch_items: PREFLIGHT: Confirm cleanup megaplan (payment-subsystem-follow-up-20260410-2339) has landed on main before starting Phase A (T9-T11). Check for PaymentWorkerCog/PaymentUICog split and PaymentActor-based confirm_payment signature. If not landed, pause Phase A; Phases B/C/D/E/F can proceed against the pre-cleanup shape with minor path adjustments.

FLAG-001 (addressed): force_reconcile_payment_to_confirmed/force_reconcile_payment_to_failed INTENTIONALLY bypass the normal mark_payment_* strict guards. Accountability is the tx_signature_history append + WARNING log. Do NOT weaken the strict guards on mark_payment_confirmed/mark_payment_failed — they remain the normal-flow contract.

FLAG-002 (addressed): The dispatcher at tools.py:3134-3139 MUST pass bot to both retry_payment and release_payment. Missing this wiring means payment_service is unreachable and the reconcile gate is a no-op.

FLAG-003 (addressed): D11/T13 reproduction MUST target the DOWNSTREAM _start_admin_payment_flow / handle_payment_result call sites, not execute_initiate_payment. Use distinct source_channel_ids to bypass the intent dedupe layer. Targeting the wrong layer would falsely 'prove safe'.

CONCERN A (addressed): NO admin_chat LLM tool named execute_payment_resolve (or any equivalent) may exist. /payment-resolve is slash-command-only. Exposing reconcile via the LLM path would re-open the prompt-injection attack surface closed by the hardening megaplan.

CONCERN B (addressed): T12 verdict comment MUST cite FOR UPDATE SKIP LOCKED + Postgres read-committed isolation as the proof. The fake-DB test is a 'logical-correctness sanity check'. Phrase 'proven safe by this test' is FORBIDDEN — use 'proven safe by the documented Postgres semantics; this test is a sanity check'.

ADMIN-DM APPROVAL MEGAPLAN: T14 (hypothesis state machine) should ideally run after the admin-DM megaplan lands so it sees the ADMIN_DM PaymentActorKind. If that megaplan is not yet landed, T14 should still run but note the missing actor kind in a comment.

B4 150s BLOCKHASH HEURISTIC and D10 150s RECLAIM WINDOW are independent thresholds that coincidentally share the same value. Keep them as SEPARATE CONSTANTS in code — do not DRY them to a single shared constant.
D11 FIX DIRECTION: If collision is confirmed, use int(time.time() * 1000) for millisecond precision. UUID suffix is explicitly REJECTED because it would break idempotency on legitimate replay paths.
RECONCILIATION SURFACE: /payment-resolve callback must gate on ADMIN_USER_ID INSIDE the callback body (match repo's admin gating pattern, not a permission decorator).

A3 SCOPE LIMIT: src/common/redaction.py only consolidates the three identical _redact_wallet helpers. Do NOT touch admin_chat/tools.py's _redact_wallet_address / _redact_wallet_row / _redact_payment_row — different signatures, separate follow-up.
F15 SCHEDULER: Ship only the script. No CI workflow file, no Railway config change. Scheduler wire-up is documented in runbook as operator decision.
DEBT WATCH: admin-payments-intent-state debt (button confirmation path updates admin_payment_intents only indirectly via handle_payment_result) — T13's D11 fix should not make this worse.
DEBT WATCH: payment-service-confirmation debt (null-recipient DM wiring bundled with cap-breach DM wiring) — T7's DM fallback refactor should preserve existing null-recipient log-and-return behavior.
REFACTOR PRESERVATION: T9 is strictly behavior-preserving. Public signature, return values, AND log messages must stay identical. Existing tests must pass without modification — that is the gate.

RPC-DOWN SENTINEL BRIDGE: T8's 'rpc_unreachable' must be emitted ONLY on connection-family exceptions. A confirmation wait-budget timeout (RPC responsive) stays as 'timeout'. T2's reconcile_with_chain treats 'rpc_unreachable' as keep_in_hold.

sense_checks.question: Do force_reconcile_payment_to_confirmed and force_reconcile_payment_to_failed both accept allowed_statuses=['submitted','processing','failed','manual_hold'], reject pending_confirmation/queued/cancelled, append to tx_signature_history with reconcile_* reason, and log at WARNING? Are mark_payment_confirmed/mark_payment_failed's strict guards unchanged?

Does reconcile_with_chain cover all six decision branches (reconciled_confirmed, reconciled_failed, allow_requeue, keep_in_hold, not_applicable, rpc_unreachable→keep_in_hold) with a unit test per branch? Does the 150s blockhash heuristic use now - submitted_at?

Does the dispatcher at tools.py:3134-3139 pass bot for BOTH retry_payment and release_payment? Do both tools fail closed when payment_service is missing? Is reconcile_with_chain called BEFORE any DB mutation, and are all five decision branches tested per tool?

Is /payment-resolve gated on ADMIN_USER_ID inside the callback? Is there ZERO execute_payment_resolve or equivalent in src/features/admin_chat/tools.py? Does a grep for 'payment_resolve' in admin_chat/tools.py return nothing?
Does migrate_legacy_provider_rows handle all three branches (grants→solana_grants, admin_chat→solana_payouts, unknown→manual_hold)? Is it called from worker cog_load and tested with fake DB?

Does _get_dynamic_priority_fee compute the 75th percentile of non-zero fees, clamp to [floor, ceiling], fall back to static floor on exception, and log the chosen value? Is it wired into send_sol at the right line? Do the mock tests cover percentile math, both clamps, and fallback?
Does _deliver_admin_alert try DM first, fall back to ADMIN_FALLBACK_CHANNEL_ID on Forbidden/rate limit, and log at ERROR when both fail? Are both success and failure DM paths routed through it?

Is 'rpc_unreachable' emitted ONLY on connection-family exceptions, not on legitimate confirmation timeouts? Are both _confirm_submitted_payment and recover_inflight updated to branch on it with a distinct manual_hold reason? Does reconcile_with_chain treat it as keep_in_hold?

Is request_payment now a ~40-line orchestrator calling exactly 5 named helpers? Are the public signature and all log messages byte-identical to before? Does the existing test suite pass with ZERO modifications?
Does PaymentService.__init__ raise ValueError below MIN_TEST_LAMPORTS=2_000_000? Was the duplicate guard at main.py:173-184 removed while keeping the env var read? Is the new unit test covering this?

Is redact_wallet defined EXACTLY ONCE in src/common/redaction.py? Are payment_service.py, payment_cog.py, and db_handler.py importing it via alias? Are admin_chat/tools.py's _redact_wallet_* helpers UNTOUCHED?

Does the verdict comment at the top of tests/test_payment_race.py cite FOR UPDATE SKIP LOCKED and Postgres read-committed isolation as the proof? Does it explicitly label the test as 'logical-correctness sanity check, not a proof of DB-level concurrency safety'? Is the phrase 'proven safe by this test' ABSENT?

Does the D11 reproduction use distinct source_channel_ids to bypass intent dedupe and drive the downstream _start_admin_payment_flow path? If collision confirmed, does producer_ref use int(time.time() * 1000) NOT a UUID suffix? Is there a verdict comment stating the outcome?

Is hypothesis added to requirements.txt? Does the RuleBasedStateMachine include a reconcile_with_chain rule? Are all 5 invariants checked after every rule, with the reconciliation edge explicitly NOT treated as a backward terminal transition?

Does docs/payments.md include the state machine ASCII diagram with reconciliation edges, the PaymentActorKind×producer table, the reconciliation contract (why force_reconcile_* bypasses guards and that it's only reachable via /payment-resolve), and file:line pointers? Is it ≤400 lines?

Does docs/runbook-payments.md cover all six scenarios (manual_hold, failed, ghost-verified wallet, DM undelivered, RPC down, budget cap)? Does the scheduler wire-up section explicitly flag the choice as an operator decision? Is there NO CI workflow file shipped?

Does scripts/check_payment_invariants.py implement all 5 checks (a-e)? Does it exit nonzero on violation? Does the synthetic-violation fixture test each check? Is there NO CI workflow file shipped alongside?
Does SolanaClient.confirm_tx emit structured logs at all THREE branches (confirmed, not_found, errored) with event='tx_confirm_decision' in extra? Are the test patches asserting the extra dict shape?

Does the full pytest suite pass green? Do the Phase D verdict comments cite Postgres semantics for T12 and report T13 outcome clearly? Does check_payment_invariants.py exit nonzero on synthetic violations? Was the throwaway reconcile repro script deleted after confirming the fix?

meta_commentary: Execution order: Phase B (T1-T5) → Phase C (T6-T8) → Phase A (T9-T11) → Phase D (T12-T14) → Phase E (T15-T16) → Phase F (T17-T18) → Final verify (T19). Phase A MUST run after B/C because the new tests defend the refactor, and behavior-preservation is the gate for T9. Key judgment calls: (1) T1's force_reconcile_* primitives are NEW and intentionally bypass the normal mark_payment_* strict guards — this is a deliberate correctness choice, not a bug; the tx_signature_history append and WARNING log are the accountability mechanism. (2) T4's /payment-resolve is slash-command-ONLY; there must be NO execute_payment_resolve admin_chat LLM tool — this preserves the prompt-injection closure from the hardening megaplan. (3) T12's verdict MUST cite FOR UPDATE SKIP LOCKED + Postgres read-committed isolation as the proof, NOT the fake-DB test. The phrase 'proven safe by this test' is forbidden; the correct phrasing is 'proven safe by the documented Postgres semantics; this test is a sanity check'. (4) T13's reproduction must target the DOWNSTREAM _start_admin_payment_flow / handle_payment_result call sites, NOT execute_initiate_payment's intent-dedupe layer — use distinct source_channel_ids. (5) T13 fix uses int(time.time()*1000) for millisecond precision; UUID suffix is REJECTED because it would break legitimate replay idempotency. (6) T11 does NOT unify admin_chat/tools.py's _redact_wallet_address etc — separate follow-up. (7) T17 ships the script only; scheduler wire-up is documented as operator decision in T16's runbook, no CI file or Railway config. (8) Preflight check: if the cleanup megaplan has not landed, Phase A must wait. Phase B/C/D can proceed against the pre-cleanup shape with minor path adjustments in T7 (payment_cog.py vs PaymentWorkerCog) and T5 (cog_load target). (9) The admin-DM approval megaplan should ideally land before T14 so hypothesis sees the ADMIN_DM actor kind; if not, note it in the test. (10) 150s appears in both T2 (blockhash expiry) and T12 (recover_inflight reclaim window) — these are independent coincidences, keep as SEPARATE constants. (11) T19's throwaway reproduction script is required by the finalize spec — write one reconcile-with-chain end-to-end scenario, run it, delete it.

plan_steps_covered.plan_step_summary: Phase B Step 1: Add force_reconcile_* DB primitives with broad allowed_statuses and history append
Phase B Step 2: Add PaymentService.reconcile_with_chain with six decision branches
Phase B Step 3: Thread bot/payment_service into execute_retry_payment and execute_release_payment; gate on reconcile_with_chain
Phase B Step 4: Add /payment-resolve slash command (no LLM tool equivalent)
Phase B Step 5: Legacy provider='solana' row migration at worker startup
Phase C Step 6: Dynamic priority fees (75th percentile + clamp + fallback)
Phase C Step 7: Admin DM fallback to ADMIN_FALLBACK_CHANNEL_ID with ERROR log on double failure
Phase C Step 8: 'rpc_unreachable' sentinel distinct from 'timeout' in providers and payment service
Phase A Step 9: Decompose request_payment into 5 named helpers with zero behavior change
Phase A Step 10: Move rent-exempt guard into PaymentService.__init__
Phase A Step 11: Consolidate _redact_wallet into new src/common/redaction.py
Phase D Step 12: recover_inflight race — formal Postgres-semantics verdict + sanity check test
Phase D Step 13: Concurrent admin payment collision reproduction at downstream call sites + millisecond fix
Phase D Step 14: hypothesis state machine tests with 5 invariants
Phase E Step 15: docs/payments.md architecture doc
Phase E Step 16: docs/runbook-payments.md operator runbook with scheduler-wire-up section
Phase F Step 17: scripts/check_payment_invariants.py with 5 checks and synthetic-violation test
Phase F Step 18: tx_confirm_decision structured log at three branches in SolanaClient.confirm_tx
Final verification: per-phase pytest + full suite + invariants script + throwaway repro

validation.completeness_notes: All 18 plan steps are mapped 1:1 to tasks T1-T18, plus T19 as the mandatory final verification task per the finalize spec. Task count (19) is proportional to the substantial scope — each step involves its own files, tests, and judgment calls. T19 bundles the validation-order commands from the plan into one final sweep. No orphan tasks. Preflight check (confirming cleanup megaplan landing) is encoded as a watch_item rather than a task because it is a conditional gate, not a work item.

baseline_test_note: Baseline tests were not executed in this finalize step — finalize produces the briefing document only. The executor must run the baseline command before starting T1 to capture any pre-existing failures that are not caused by this plan's changes. T19 prescribes the full validation sweep after implementation.
